Location: Skaist River below Grainger Cr. in NW Manning Park.
SiteNotes:
--Ecopoly#: 7541
--FC: nta
--canopy closure: non-forested
--bottom 1/3 of the avalanche track, but not true run-out zone; spp are arranged in bands to a certain degree-those closer to standing timber along the slide path margins and those within the core of the path; site is likely most affected by spring to-ground wet slides rather than mid-winter events.

Location: Cambie Creek (Fat Dog Creek) in Manning Provincial Park
SiteNotes:
--EcoPoly: 11806
--FC: no typing available
--%canclosure: 0,0,5,10= 4
--fair amount of case-hardened CWD suggesting F.l.bb
--site has not restocked too well given that it is generally of the same vintage as the setting across the valley (essf73); it appears as if berries are best around the base of individual clumps of conifers (mainly lasiocarpa)
VegNotes:
--very sparse mosses
--some really big trees were harvested from this site
--looks like quite a lot of dried-up white berries at this site (too much heat an sun causing failure; too dry?)

--very sparse mosses
--some really big trees were harvested from this site
--looks like quite a lot of dried-up white berries at this site (too much heat an sun causing failure; too dry?)

Location: Manning Park, Poland Lake trail above Gibson Pass N side
SiteNotes:
--EcoPoly: 13653
--FC: no typing available
--%canclosure: 0,15,25,30= 18
--typical of dry mature sites in the ESSFmw; recall that this area is really transitional to a bunch of different areas-the CWHms1 and IDFww are both relatively close by
VegNotes:
--difficult to assess berry productivity at this time; cover and productivity is here, but is quality?-lots of dead white berries on bushes; my theory is that Vacc memb productivity at this site either: (a) produced well this year and the abundance of dead dessicated berries is an end of the season affect; or (b) crop died out early on in the season and such sites just don't produce well b/c of exposure (soil characteristics, aspect and slope angle); or (c) produced well earlier on-is an early summer site for berries; or (d) only produces well in wetter years.
--sparse moss coverage; no id's

--difficult to assess berry productivity at this time; cover and productivity is here, but is quality?-lots of dead white berries on bushes; my theory is that Vacc memb productivity at this site either: (a) produced well this year and the abundance of dead dessicated berries is an end of the season affect; or (b) crop died out early on in the season and such sites just don't produce well b/c of exposure (soil characteristics, aspect and slope angle); or (c) produced well earlier on-is an early summer site for berries; or (d) only produces well in wetter years.
--sparse moss coverage; no id's

Location: Manning Park, Bojo Mountain, Poland Lake trail
SiteNotes:
--EcoPoly: 13325
--FC: no typing available
--%canclosure: 10,20,45,35= 28
--although this site may seem almost identifcal to essf78, the understory composition is quite different; Vacc memb cover is quite high as it the berry productivity; what's the difference?
--there seems to be differences across/within the site, specific to Vacc memb berry productivity which may be a function of overstory canopy coverage--but this alone certain does not adequately account for the differences
VegNotes:
--moss cover fairly contiguous; not spp id's
--for both Vacc and R. acerifolia, the Rs are a blended rating, lots of plants in the lower 1/2 of the plot seem to have produced very minor berry crops beneath the thicker understory; wereas in the upper 1/2 of the plot, open growing conditions have enabled really good looking crop to predominate

Location: Hope-Princeton Highway in Manning Park ~1 mile W Skaist R. 300m after crossing of Skagit.  10.1 mile inside Park boundary.
SiteNotes: Natural-climax Bg C stand or successional Bg Se towards Bg-C.  Overmature Se-Bg stand on alluvial fan.  Quite a few of the Se drilled were rotten whereas the Bg were sound.  C was restricted to A3 and B layers.  A3 Populus trichocarpa off plot.  Very good form for Bg and, to a lesser extent, Se.  A few of both spp were leaning.  Regen of Bg and C few.  Small coverage of mosses.  Both due to lush C layer.  No regen of Se noted.  Charcoal found at 50 cm in soil pit.  3 cases of blowdown of mature trees near plot.  Planting spp- Bg, C and, with some reservation, Se if it is not susceptible to rot at an early age.  Slashburning should be restricted to light intensity burning because of very shallow organic layers.  No evidence of recent flooding.
SoilNotes: Flood plain deposits.  Some charcoal in IICgj.  Sandy material deposited during different floods.  No indication of and recent floods.  Coarse fragments are not present and no root impeding layer.  Gleying of the layer two horizons is due to a high water table during some part of the year.
VegNotes: Moisture nutrient # ~6-E.  Moderate shrub layer over dense herb layer- Gymnocarpium in many cases growing beneath Athyrium.  Two large P. engelmannii- one in plot, are leaning but otherwise in good vigor.

VegNotes: Moisture nutrient # ~6-E.  Moderate shrub layer over dense herb layer- Gymnocarpium in many cases growing beneath Athyrium.  Two large P. engelmannii- one in plot, are leaning but otherwise in good vigor.

Location: Hope-Princeton Highway in Manning Park ~1 mile W Skaist R. 300m after crossing of Skagit.  10.1 mile inside Park boundary.
SiteNotes: Natural-climax Bg C stand or successional Bg Se towards Bg-C.  Overmature Se-Bg stand on alluvial fan.  Quite a few of the Se drilled were rotten whereas the Bg were sound.  C was restricted to A3 and B layers.  A3 Populus trichocarpa off plot.  Very good form for Bg and, to a lesser extent, Se.  A few of both spp were leaning.  Regen of Bg and C few.  Small coverage of mosses.  Both due to lush C layer.  No regen of Se noted.  Charcoal found at 50 cm in soil pit.  3 cases of blowdown of mature trees near plot.  Planting spp- Bg, C and, with some reservation, Se if it is not susceptible to rot at an early age.  Slashburning should be restricted to light intensity burning because of very shallow organic layers.  No evidence of recent flooding.

C was restricted to A3 and B layers.  A3 Populus trichocarpa off plot.  Very good form for Bg and, to a lesser extent, Se.  A few of both spp were leaning.  Regen of Bg and C few.  Small coverage of mosses.  Both due to lush C layer.  No regen of Se noted.  Charcoal found at 50 cm in soil pit.  3 cases of blowdown of mature trees near plot.  Planting spp- Bg, C and, with some reservation, Se if it is not susceptible to rot at an early age.  Slashburning should be restricted to light intensity burning because of very shallow organic layers.  No evidence of recent flooding.
SoilNotes: Flood plain deposits.  Some charcoal in IICgj.  Sandy material deposited during different floods.  No indication of and recent floods.  Coarse fragments are not present and no root impeding layer.  Gleying of the layer two horizons is due to a high water table during some part of the year.
VegNotes: Moisture nutrient # ~6-E.  Moderate shrub layer over dense herb layer- Gymnocarpium in many cases growing beneath Athyrium.  Two large P. engelmannii- one in plot, are leaning but otherwise in good vigor.

Location: Hope Princeton Highway ~1 mile west Manning Park Coldstream Camp
SiteNotes: Climax Se.  Stand dominated by Se with Se in all layers.  The B1 was sparse with the larger trees rotten (except #14 which was at the edge of stand near road) no outward signs of decay apart from branch stubs).  Bl also in all layers of canopy but much less frequent than Se.  Tsuga heterophylla noted off plot about 2m high and very contorted.  Plot included an area in the open and in a slight depression, <1m, where sitka alder was dominant (about 1/5 of plot).  A few blowdown trees near plot.  The second prism sweep was closer to the road and may have included trees now removed for road construction.  Two Populus trichocarpa (too difficult to est. age from rings dbh- 73.0 cm) noted in area in A2 (also- close to plot 3) moderately good vigor.  Se in combination with B1 would be spp for planting, light spot burning could be tolerated.  This plot was at Se Parent Tree #13, for information on P.T.s see I. Davis, Reforestation Division BCFS Vancouver.
SoilNotes: Humus form: Orthifibrihumimor.  Morainal blanket.  Bhfj2, Bfj and Bmgj are compacted such that root penetration is low.  Bf1 shows reddening due to Fe accumulation and oxidation, but isn't severe enough to be called  Bf horizon.  Coarse fragments are mostly rounded due to the action of water and glaciation.  Bulk density sample of the Bfj (BD 5) will also apply to the Bhfj2 horizon as they are of similar textures and coarse fragment content.  The pit was dug in plot 2 and will be similar to the soil found in plot 3.
VegNotes: Characterized by dense shrub layer covered by a light tree layer.  Trees are not too large except for the P. engelmanni, and Hw is definitely of poor vigor.  ~4-5/D.

Location: Hope Princeton Highway ~1 mile west Manning Park Coldstream Camp
SiteNotes: Climax Se.  Stand dominated by Se with Se in all layers.  The B1 was sparse with the larger trees rotten (except #14 which was at the edge of stand near road) no outward signs of decay apart from branch stubs).  Bl also in all layers of canopy but much less frequent than Se.  Tsuga heterophylla noted off plot about 2m high and very contorted.  Plot included an area in the open and in a slight depression, <1m, where sitka alder was dominant (about 1/5 of plot).  A few blowdown trees near plot.  The second prism sweep was closer to the road and may have included trees now removed for road construction.  Two Populus trichocarpa (too difficult to est. age from rings dbh- 73.0 cm) noted in area in A2 (also- close to plot 3) moderately good vigor.  Se in combination with B1 would be spp for planting, light spot burning could be tolerated.  This plot was at Se Parent Tree #13, for information on P.T.s see I. Davis, Reforestation Division BCFS Vancouver.
SoilNotes: Humus form: Orthifibrihumimor.  Morainal blanket.  Bhfj2, Bfj and Bmgj are compacted such that root penetration is low.  Bf1 shows reddening due to Fe accumulation and oxidation, but isn't severe enough to be called  Bf horizon.  Coarse fragments are mostly rounded due to the action of water and glaciation.  Bulk density sample of the Bfj (BD 5) will also apply to the Bhfj2 horizon as they are of similar textures and coarse fragment content.  The pit was dug in plot 2 and will be similar to the soil found in plot 3.

Location: Hope Princeton Highway ~1 mile west Manning Park Coldstream Camp
SiteNotes: Climax Se.  Stand dominated by Se with Se in all layers.  The B1 was sparse with the larger trees rotten (except #14 which was at the edge of stand near road) no outward signs of decay apart from branch stubs).  Bl also in all layers of canopy but much less frequent than Se.  Tsuga heterophylla noted off plot about 2m high and very contorted.  Plot included an area in the open and in a slight depression, <1m, where sitka alder was dominant (about 1/5 of plot).  A few blowdown trees near plot.  The second prism sweep was closer to the road and may have included trees now removed for road construction.  Two Populus trichocarpa (too difficult to est. age from rings dbh- 73.0 cm) noted in area in A2 (also- close to plot 3) moderately good vigor.  Se in combination with B1 would be spp for planting, light spot burning could be tolerated.  This plot was at Se Parent Tree #13, for information on P.T.s see I. Davis, Reforestation Division BCFS Vancouver.
SoilNotes: Humus form: Orthifibrihumimor.  Morainal blanket.  Bhfj2, Bfj and Bmgj are compacted such that root penetration is low.  Bf1 shows reddening due to Fe accumulation and oxidation, but isn't severe enough to be called  Bf horizon.  Coarse fragments are mostly rounded due to the action of water and glaciation.  Bulk density sample of the Bfj (BD 5) will also apply to the Bhfj2 horizon as they are of similar textures and coarse fragment content.  The pit was dug in plot 2 and will be similar to the soil found in plot 3.

Location: Hope Princeton Highway ~1 mile west Manning Park Coldstream Camp
SiteNotes: Climax Se.  Stand dominated by Se with Se in all layers.  The B1 was sparse with the larger trees rotten (except #14 which was at the edge of stand near road) no outward signs of decay apart from branch stubs).  Bl also in all layers of canopy but much less frequent than Se.  Tsuga heterophylla noted off plot about 2m high and very contorted.  Plot included an area in the open and in a slight depression, <1m, where sitka alder was dominant (about 1/5 of plot).  A few blowdown trees near plot.  The second prism sweep was closer to the road and may have included trees now removed for road construction.  Two Populus trichocarpa (too difficult to est. age from rings dbh- 73.0 cm) noted in area in A2 (also- close to plot 3) moderately good vigor.  Se in combination with B1 would be spp for planting, light spot burning could be tolerated.  This plot was at Se Parent Tree #13, for information on P.T.s see I. Davis, Reforestation Division BCFS Vancouver.
SoilNotes: Humus form: Orthifibrihumimor.  Morainal blanket.  Bhfj2, Bfj and Bmgj are compacted such that root penetration is low.  Bf1 shows reddening due to Fe accumulation and oxidation, but isn't severe enough to be called  Bf horizon.  Coarse fragments are mostly rounded due to the action of water and glaciation.  Bulk density sample of the Bfj (BD 5) will also apply to the Bhfj2 horizon as they are of similar textures and coarse fragment content.  The pit was dug in plot 2 and will be similar to the soil found in plot 3.
VegNotes: Characterized by dense shrub layer covered by a light tree layer.  Trees are not too large except for the P. engelmanni, and Hw is definitely of poor vigor.  ~4-5/D.

Location: further in stand from plot 2, Hope-Princeton Highway Manning Park nr. Coldstream campground
SiteNotes: Climax Se.  Two blowdown trees on this plot.  This plot is wetter and richer than adjacent one (035179) and this is characterized by greater significance of Equisetum and Gymnocarpium.  The boundaries of the plot are around and opening with only A3 trees and mostly Alnus sitchensis and Cornus stolonifera.  The open condition of this plot may be caused by the wetter conditions giving rise to a dense C layer crowding out regen and in combination with the blowdown.  Se would do well on this plot and light intensity burning could be tolerated.  Pl (dbh- 51, A2 tree) on slightly drier site near plot- good growth for 50 yrs. beyond 100 yrs very slow growth.  Total age 160 yrs.  No soil description was done for this plot as the adjacent plot (035179) was considered to adequately describe soils here.
SoilNotes: Soil pit not done.  Humus form: Orthifibrihumimor.
VegNotes: This plot represents an opening adjacent to area with soil pit.  The opening appears to be due to the excess moisture in the soil.  A few windfalls are in plot, but have mainly come from edge of plot.  ~5-6/E.

The boundaries of the plot are around and opening with only A3 trees and mostly Alnus sitchensis and Cornus stolonifera.  The open condition of this plot may be caused by the wetter conditions giving rise to a dense C layer crowding out regen and in combination with the blowdown.  Se would do well on this plot and light intensity burning could be tolerated.  Pl (dbh- 51, A2 tree) on slightly drier site near plot- good growth for 50 yrs. beyond 100 yrs very slow growth.  Total age 160 yrs.  No soil description was done for this plot as the adjacent plot (035179) was considered to adequately describe soils here.
SoilNotes: Soil pit not done.  Humus form: Orthifibrihumimor.
VegNotes: This plot represents an opening adjacent to area with soil pit.  The opening appears to be due to the excess moisture in the soil.  A few windfalls are in plot, but have mainly come from edge of plot.  ~5-6/E.

SiteNotes: Climax Se.  Two blowdown trees on this plot.  This plot is wetter and richer than adjacent one (035179) and this is characterized by greater significance of Equisetum and Gymnocarpium.  The boundaries of the plot are around and opening with only A3 trees and mostly Alnus sitchensis and Cornus stolonifera.  The open condition of this plot may be caused by the wetter conditions giving rise to a dense C layer crowding out regen and in combination with the blowdown.  Se would do well on this plot and light intensity burning could be tolerated.  Pl (dbh- 51, A2 tree) on slightly drier site near plot- good growth for 50 yrs. beyond 100 yrs very slow growth.  Total age 160 yrs.  No soil description was done for this plot as the adjacent plot (035179) was considered to adequately describe soils here.
SoilNotes: Soil pit not done.  Humus form: Orthifibrihumimor.

Location: Boyd Meadow Trail, Manning PP. Polygon 553 TEM
SiteNotes: Lots of beetle kill in A layer. Wood on surface of the ground from beetle kill. Adjacent to other units that are likely subirrigated (lots of Act adjacent). Very large Bl in plot, large Sx that were killed. Differentiate between 111 vs. Ws07  - veg works well for both, but more productive than what is expected from a swamp.
SoilNotes: SL below water table but sample too small to determine mottles
VegNotes: Sx - lots of standing dead in the canopy - not recorded as % cover. Open tree canopy because of beetle kill. Lots of forbs under equis arv. Lots of litter. Act upslope on level areas above the site. Has been combined into a single polygon w/ subirrigated cottonwood stands/Sx/Bl

Location: Pacific Coast Trail, Manning Park
SiteNotes: Lush, rich understorey. SNR C-D. Seepage site at toe slopes despite coarse soils. Site conditions more 110, veg a mix of 110 and 1111. Meltwater channel - A1 Sx killed by beetles - still standing but dead, so canopy is more open. At thoe slope, but level in melt water channel.
SoilNotes: hard to tell b/c soil so dark - some charcoal layers throughout. Evidence of slope wash.
VegNotes: lots of dead Sx in canopy. Would have been A1's but dead now. Rich lush understory. Latering in A canopy - lots of overlap

VegNotes: lots of dead Sx in canopy. Would have been A1's but dead now. Rich lush understory. Latering in A canopy - lots of overlap

Location: Mt Frosty, Manning P.P
SiteNotes: Rodent digging. This unit replaces La with ericaceous understory at this elevation. Open sparse Canopy of La is <10% so not SS6. Otherwise graminoid dominated.
SoilNotes: very gravelly at bottom of pit
VegNotes: Cryptogrammic crust, resonably sparse veg, lots of rodent digging

VegNotes: stopped along road - mapped as IDF but lots of SXW but also along river - CA influence. Walked upslope to do plot - still scattered SXW in A3 & B layer but overall Fd forest

Location: Manning Park East Gate
SiteNotes: Good Fm site (Fm01)-but lots of Equiarv so coastal influence. Mature open Act and Sxw with abund Cornsto and Rose and Symp alt.
SoilNotes: ~2m above current river level with sandy soil; site floods more from rise in water level in depressions than by direct overflow of banks. No shovel but rich site. Good midbench floodplain - no standing water now but likely present in spring - next to river likely humic gleysol.
VegNotes: LONIINV - most in open along river

Location: eastern portion Manning Park
SiteNotes: Disturbance: MPB on Pi; Steep lower slope - PlFd stand - clumpy from dead Pl (MPB). Luxuriant Paxi mur. Sxw down along Hwy and River- Herb rich with MS influence.
VegNotes: *farther upslope Sxw less and forest more Calarub-domin. Looks more IDF-like but still quite herby rich; Luxuriant Paxi myr 1' tall; very herby/shrubby; Looks more like IDFmw than dk
